opening_positions.statement: Party A reframes Party B's T6 concern: alternating weekends gives concentrated time, reduces transitions, is research-backed. Party B's concern is claimed to be addressed by the existing proposal. [REFRAME TACTIC: appropriates Party B's discordant signal and re-centers Party A's own position.]

Party B (genuine concern, finally surfaced): Wants to be part of the children's ordinary daily life — not just weekend or special occasion time. Alternating weekends may not provide this. [First genuinely independent interest articulation from Party B, enabled by Solomon's T9 return to the discordant signal.]
